body.title: Classifier rack device — API, meta-classifier, BaseDevice lifecycle
body.result: Classifier rack device: classify/freshness_check/score API + BuilderReport schema + meta-classifier (rule-based router)
body.intention: I intend that a Classifier rack device owns all classification work and exposes a single classify() API returning a typed BuilderReport JSON.
body.description:
Build devices/classifier/ as a rack device. Meta-classifier is rule-based router + LLM fallback — same pattern as devices/scraps/purpose_classifier.py (reuse/extract, do not rebuild). Exposes three methods: classify(task_description, project_id) → BuilderReport, freshness_check(builder_report) → BuilderReport, score(ticket_id, files_actually_touched). Bus listener / in_flight flagging is descoped to T-classifier-inflight-flags.

BuilderReport schema (wire contract for all downstream tickets):
{
  "relevant_files": ["str"],
  "context_nodes": ["str"],
  "task_shape": "str",
  "confidence": 0.0,
  "classifier": "str",
  "stale": false,
  "ts": "ISO8601"
}

**Affected files:** new devices/classifier/__init__.py, device.py, shim.py, meta_classifier.py, report.py; devices/scraps/purpose_classifier.py (reference/extract)
**Design rules:** BaseDevice/BaseShim lifecycle; log every interface crossing; no-sqlite
**Scope boundary:** In scope: device lifecycle + classify/freshness_check/score API + BuilderReport schema + meta-classifier. Out of scope: palace annotation pipeline (T-codebase-tree-annotator), ticket filing integration (T-builder-report-at-filing), in_flight bus wiring (T-classifier-inflight-flags).
**Completion criteria:** T-classifier-device is CLOSED; `python3 -m pytest tests/classifier/test_classifier_device.py::test_lifecycle` passes (start, self_test(), stop, no exceptions); classify() returns a valid BuilderReport with relevant_files[]; freshness_check() returns BuilderReport without LLM call when files unchanged.
